title: The Flag Must Not Fall
description: On pages 17–18, this part focuses on the flag must not fall, showing the key character choices and conflict behind this chapter beat.
scene: The Flag Must Not Fall. Luffy understands the meaning of Hiriluk’s flag and refuses to let Wapol treat it like a decoration. The scene description focuses on what the reader needs to understand before the dialogue continues.

dialogue.speaker: Luffy
dialogue.text: I'll take on the big-mouthed hippo then!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: You think you can beat me!? Ha! Get a load of this stupid animal!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Damn you, Hag... let me... fight...!

dialogue.speaker: Nami
dialogue.text: Stay put and enjoy the show. Push yourself anymore and you won't be able to walk again, y'hear!?

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: My lil' Reindeer can handle himself when the time comes.

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: My name is Tony Tony Chopperr!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: The world's greatest doctor gave me that name!

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: Even if Doc would forgive you brute,

dialogue.speaker: Dr. Kureha
dialogue.text: I'll NEVER forgive you for mocking him!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #1
dialogue.text: The one begging for forgiveness will be you, you dumb monster!

summary: This part highlights the flag must not fall and moves the chapter forward through its central emotional or plot turn.
